title: glechoma hederacea var. grandis
intro: Glechoma hederacea var. grandis, also known as large creeping thyme or large leaved glechoma, is a perennial herb in the mint family that is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Glechoma hederacea var. grandis have been valued for their ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as their potential to soothe digestive problems and reduce anxiety. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
